title: Running `buildah` in gVisor
body: As `kaniko` is archived since 2025-05-03 is there a fix for the (now locked) issue https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2099?

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Were you able to get this working? I gave it a shot but I get `cannot set user namespace` errors. Strangely enough, the first `podman build` inside my container works but then it breaks for subsequent calls

Sorry no. We used gVisor for our GitLab (instance) runner, but we decided to go a complete other way and now each pipeline job has its own VM which will be destroyed afterwards and thus we don’t need gVisor anymore.

> Sorry no. We used gVisor for our GitLab (instance) runner, but we decided to go a complete other way and now each pipeline job has its own VM which will be destroyed afterwards and thus we don’t need gVisor anymore.

Ah fair enough. I actually found that the current gVisor supports `buildah bud` out of the box now. I'm having issues with rootless`podman build` though https://github.com/google/gvisor/issues/13314
Though we won't change the current set up with our GitLab runners, the PR fixing the `setns` might still come in handy at other places. Thanks for taking the issue to gVisor.
